text: Risk management is an important process for any business or project. It begins with identifying risks, which means looking for possible problems that could cause harm or delays. These might be financial issues, technical failures, or even natural disasters. Once risks are identified, mitigation strategies are developed. These are actions taken to reduce the chance of a risk happening or to lessen its impact. However, not all risks can be prevented, so contingency plans are necessary. These are backup plans that are ready to use if a risk does occur. Finally, continuous monitoring is crucial. By regularly checking for new risks and reviewing existing plans, organisations can stay prepared and respond quickly when needed.
